title: Atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular rate

items.text: Have you been diagnosed with atrial fibrillation before, and if so, does this feel like your usual episodes or is it your first time?
choices: Known paroxysmal atrial fibrillation, similar to prior episodes
Known persistent or permanent atrial fibrillation

items.text: Do you have any chest pain along with the palpitations?
Are you short of breath along with the palpitations?
Have you felt like you were going to pass out, or did you actually pass out?
Have you had a fever or felt sick before this started?
Did you binge drink alcohol recently?
Have you noticed heat intolerance, tremor, or unexplained weight loss, or do you have a known thyroid problem?
choices: New heat intolerance, tremor, or unintentional weight loss

items.text: Are you on a blood thinner for your heart rhythm, and have you been taking it exactly as prescribed?
Have you had any unusual bruising or bleeding while on your blood thinner?
Do you have high blood pressure, diabetes, known blood vessel disease, or have you had a stroke or mini-stroke before?
choices: Hypertension, diabetes, or known vascular disease
Prior stroke or transient ischemic attack

items.text: Have you had surgery recently or been sick with another acute illness?
Have you been diagnosed with Wolff-Parkinson-White syndrome or another abnormal heart electrical pathway?
Have you used any stimulant drugs or had a lot more caffeine than usual recently?
choices: No recent stimulant or excess caffeine intake
Recent stimulant use or excess caffeine intake

items.text: Do you need extra pillows to breathe at night, wake up gasping for air, or have new leg swelling?
Assess heart rate and rhythm regularity.
Assess hemodynamic stability.
Assess jugular venous pressure and peripheral edema.
choices: Normal jugular venous pressure, no edema
Elevated jugular venous pressure or peripheral edema

items.text: Auscultate the lungs for crackles.
Assess the thyroid for goiter or tremor.
Compare the apical heart rate to the radial pulse rate for a pulse deficit.
Assess mental status and perfusion.
choices: Altered mental status or poor perfusion

items.text: Assess peripheral pulses for an acutely cool or pulseless limb suggesting embolism.
Inspect the skin for ecchymosis related to anticoagulation.
Inspect and palpate the calves for asymmetric swelling or tenderness.
